Selecting a distance option: Updates internal form state for distanceMiles; on Search submit, this value is written to the URL parameter distanceMiles and sent to searchClinicLocations.

Clicking the Search button (form submit): Prevents default page reload, updates URL parameters for zipCode and distanceMiles (and preserves/includes requireFreeParking, requireOpenSaturday, amenities, hours, sortBy), then triggers a searchClinicLocations call using the URL-derived values.

Toggling free parking filter: When enabled, sets requireFreeParking=true in the URL and calls searchClinicLocations with requireFreeParking set accordingly; when disabled, removes or sets requireFreeParking=false in the URL and calls searchClinicLocations again.

Selecting other amenity filters: Updates an amenities URL parameter to represent the selected amenity values; after searchClinicLocations returns the full locations list for the current backend filters, the client filters the array so that only locations whose amenities field includes all selected amenity values are rendered.

Toggling Saturday hours filter: When enabled, sets requireOpenSaturday=true in the URL and calls searchClinicLocations with requireOpenSaturday=true; when disabled, removes or sets requireOpenSaturday=false and calls searchClinicLocations again.

Selecting other hours filters: Updates a hours URL parameter to represent selected hours options; the client applies best-effort filtering on the locations array based on available fields (e.g., openSaturday, hoursSummary) before rendering.

Changing sort option: Updates sortBy in the URL, triggers a new call to searchClinicLocations with the chosen sort key, and optionally applies corresponding client-side sorting to the locations array before display.
Clicking a clinic card: Reads the clicked card’s locationId and navigates to location_detail.html?locationId=<locationId>.

Page load / URL change: The controller parses query parameters, initializes all filter and sort components accordingly, calls getLocationFilterOptions once to populate filter choices, and then calls searchClinicLocations when a valid zipCode is present or when the user submits the search form.

Handling no results: If searchClinicLocations returns totalResults equal to zero, the results list component displays an empty-state message and may suggest adjusting filters; no static example locations are shown.
page_functionality.state_logic: The single source of truth for page state is the URL query string. On initial page load and on every URL change (including browser navigation), the controller component parses these parameters: zipCode (string): if present, pre-fills the ZIP input and is passed to searchClinicLocations.zipCode. distanceMiles (number): if present, selects the corresponding distance option in the distance dropdown (using getLocationFilterOptions.distanceOptions to find a matching miles value) and is passed to searchClinicLocations.distanceMiles; if not present, distanceMiles is either omitted or passed as null so backend defaults apply. requireFreeParking (boolean): if present and truthy, the free parking filter control is checked and searchClinicLocations.requireFreeParking is set to true; if absent or false, the control is unchecked and requireFreeParking is set to false or omitted. requireOpenSaturday (boolean): if present and truthy, the Saturday hours filter is checked and searchClinicLocations.requireOpenSaturday is set to true; otherwise it is treated as false or omitted. sortBy (string): if present, the sort control selects the corresponding option and this value is passed through to searchClinicLocations.sortBy. If sortBy is missing, the sort control reflects a default choice and sortBy is omitted or left undefined in the API call, allowing backend default ordering. Additional URL parameters used only on this page include: amenities (string): an encoded representation (such as a comma-separated list) of selected amenity filter values from getLocationFilterOptions.amenities; this parameter is not sent to searchClinicLocations but is used to filter the returned locations client-side based on each location's amenities array. hours (string): an encoded representation of selected hours filter values from getLocationFilterOptions.hoursOptions; this parameter is not sent to searchClinicLocations except where a value explicitly maps to Saturday and thus requireOpenSaturday; otherwise it controls client-side filtering based on openSaturday and hoursSummary. Whenever the user changes a filter, search, or sort control, the components construct a new query string reflecting the updated state, push it to the browser URL, and notify the controller. The controller then re-derives state from the URL and issues a fresh call to searchClinicLocations with zipCode, distanceMiles, requireFreeParking, requireOpenSaturday, and sortBy derived solely from the URL. The results list is always rendered using the most recent searchClinicLocations response, further filtered client-side by amenities and hours according to the amenities and hours URL parameters. No persistent state is stored outside the URL and in-memory component state for the current view; there is no use of localStorage or similar mechanisms.

components.functionality: Coordinates initial data loading, synchronizes URL parameters with component state, and orchestrates calls to getLocationFilterOptions and searchClinicLocations.
Provides the ZIP code input and distance dropdown, initializes from URL parameters, and triggers searches when the form is submitted.
Displays amenity filters including free parking; maps free parking to requireFreeParking for backend filtering and applies other amenity filters client-side.
Displays hours filters and maps relevant options to requireOpenSaturday for backend filtering, with additional hours filters applied client-side where possible.
Allows the user to choose the sorting order (e.g., by distance); passes the associated sort key to the controller and syncs with the sortBy URL parameter.
Shows the number of matching clinic locations and a concise summary of active search and filter criteria.
Indicates when a search is in progress, based on whether a searchClinicLocations request is pending.
Renders a list of clinic location cards based on the locations returned by searchClinicLocations, further filtered client-side according to amenities and hours URL parameters.
Represents a single clinic location in the list, displaying its basic details and handling navigation to the detail page when clicked.
Displays a message and guidance when searchClinicLocations.totalResults is zero for the current filters.
Provides helper logic to read from and write to the URL query string whenever any search, filter, or sort control changes, ensuring all components derive their state from the URL.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column desktop grid with a centered max-width content container (~1100–1200px) inside full-width color bands.
layout.layout_pattern: The page follows the mockup’s simple top-to-bottom flow: a global header (logo left, nav right) and full-width hero with centered ink-cloud page title at the top, then a vertically stacked Locations Finder experience beneath. Within the main content, the search form is the primary focal point and is placed centrally just below the hero title, followed by a pair of horizontal filter bars, then a compact results toolbar (summary on the left, sort control on the right), and finally a vertically scrolling list of clinic cards. Invisible controller/state components wrap and orchestrate the visible elements without taking visual space.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page follows the mockup’s simple top-to-bottom flow: a global header (logo left, nav right) and full-width hero with centered ink-cloud page title at the top, then a vertically stacked Locations Finder experience beneath. Within the main content, the search form is the primary focal point and is placed centrally just below the hero title, followed by a pair of horizontal filter bars, then a compact results toolbar (summary on the left, sort control on the right), and finally a vertically scrolling list of clinic cards. Invisible controller/state components wrap and orchestrate the visible elements without taking visual space.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The header occupies roughly the top 15–20% of the viewport, with a tall hero band below taking most of the remaining initial view, consistent with the mockup. The ink-cloud and page title dominate the upper hero area, while the lower portion of the hero is reserved for the centered search form. Below the hero, the filters and results sit on a white background within a centered 1100–1200px max-width container, with generous 64–96px vertical gaps between major sections (search block, filters, results) and 24–32px gaps between related elements, maintaining a calm, breathable layout.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content density stays low, with few elements per horizontal row and ample padding around each group. The search form is separated from the hero title and filters with comfortable 32–48px vertical space; the filter bars are visually grouped but not crowded, and the results toolbar and list use clear spacing between cards. Cards are single-column, full-width within the content container to avoid visual noise. Overall, the layout emphasizes large touch targets, 16–24px internal padding, and generous margins, supporting a therapeutic, non-cluttered reading experience for parents and educators.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow mirrors the mockup: the eye starts at the top-left logo, scans right along the header navigation, then drops down to the strong central focal point—the ink-cloud hero title. From there, the eye moves straight down to the centrally aligned search form, then horizontally across the filter bars, then down to the results summary on the left and over to the sort control on the right, before continuing down through the vertical stack of clinic cards. This combines the header’s F-pattern with a strong central hero focus, then guides users into a clear, linear search-and-results flow.

layout.overall_description: Below the site’s global header, the Locations Finder page opens with a full-width slate-blue hero band, within which a dark, organic ink-cloud shape is centered horizontally and vertically in the upper half. The page title sits in two lines of calm, confident type within this ink-cloud, echoing the mockup’s primary focal element. In the lower half of the hero, aligned to the center of the page, a wide, white search form card spans about 60% of the page width (capped by the 1100–1200px content container), with generous breathing room above and below so it feels anchored but not cramped. As you scroll below the hero, the background transitions to white while the content remains centered within the same max-width container. First come two soft, horizontal filter bars stacked one above the other, stretching nearly the full container width, followed by a compact toolbar row where the results summary sits on the left and the sort control aligns to the right along a shared baseline. Beneath this toolbar, a single-column list of clinic result cards descends vertically with generous 24–32px gaps between cards and 64–96px space at the bottom, giving a calm rhythm to the scan. During loading or when no results are found, that central list region is taken over by a clearly centered loading or empty state message, but the overall structure—hero, search, filters, toolbar—remains intact, ensuring a predictable and reassuring experience.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The locations-page-controller acts as an invisible structural wrapper that begins immediately below the global header and encompasses the full width of the viewport. It defines the vertical stacking order of the Locations Finder experience: first the full-width hero band (with ink-cloud title and embedded search form), then the white-background main content section containing filters, toolbar, and results. Internally, it constrains all interactive content to a centered max-width container of approximately 1100–1200px, aligned with the site’s 12-column grid, and applies the large 64–96px vertical spacing between the major sections to preserve the calm, open feel of the mockup. It does not render any visible UI itself but ensures consistent alignment, padding, and section spacing for all child components.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual structural container; full-width block in normal document flow that controls section alignment and spacing but does not appear as a distinct element.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The location-search-form is centered horizontally in the lower half of the hero section, directly beneath the ink-cloud title with approximately 32–48px of vertical space separating the bottom of the title shape and the top of the form. It appears as a wide, white card or pill-shaped bar spanning about 60% of the page width on large screens (capped at roughly 700–800px), sitting within the hero’s colored background and aligned to the same central axis as the ink-cloud. On smaller screens it expands to around 90–100% of the content width with side margins. The card uses 16–24px internal padding and arranges its fields horizontally on desktop—ZIP or city input on the left, distance selector and a prominent submit button on the right—while stacking them vertically on narrow viewports. There is generous negative space (about 32–40px) both above and below the form within the hero band so it feels like a focused, primary interaction point rather than crowded against other elements.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed position within the hero band in normal flow; centered and responsive in width, maintaining its position relative to the hero title as the viewport resizes.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The amenities-filter-bar sits immediately below the hero section in the main white-background content area, with a comfortable vertical gap of about 48–64px between the bottom of the hero (and search form) and the top of this filter bar. It is contained within the same centered 1100–1200px max-width grid and spans nearly the full content width, leaving only standard side padding (24–32px) to the edges. Visually, it appears as a low-height horizontal strip of toggleable filter chips or buttons, arranged in a single row where possible, wrapping to a second line only if necessary. The bar has 16–20px top and bottom padding to keep it airy, with about 16px vertical space separating it from the hours-filter-bar below, making them feel related but distinct. The background remains white or a very light neutral to maintain the clean, therapeutic look, and the entire bar reads as a secondary control band that users encounter immediately after the main search.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block-level element in normal flow, anchored at the top of the main content area and stretching horizontally within the centered content container.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The hours-filter-bar is placed directly beneath the amenities-filter-bar, aligned to the same centered content container and matching it in width. A vertical gap of about 16–24px separates the two bars so they feel visually connected as a filter group while still remaining readable as separate controls. Like the amenities bar, it presents its options as a horizontal row of pills, checkboxes, or segmented buttons (e.g., ‘Open now’, ‘Evenings’, ‘Weekends’), with 16–20px internal vertical padding for a comfortable tap area. On desktop, the controls are left-aligned within the bar, leaving whitespace to the right; on narrow screens, they may wrap into multiple rows but remain within the same visual band. The entire filter group (amenities + hours) is followed by a larger vertical gap of about 24–32px before the results toolbar to clearly separate filtering from the output area.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block-level element stacked directly after the amenities-filter-bar; width and alignment mirror the bar above for a cohesive stripe of filters.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The sort-control is positioned within a slim toolbar row immediately below the filter bars. This toolbar sits inside the centered content container, spanning its full width, with about 24–32px of vertical space above it (from the hours-filter-bar) so it feels like the start of the results section. Within this row, the sort-control is right-aligned on desktop, occupying roughly the rightmost third of the toolbar width, opposite the results-summary on the left. It appears as a compact dropdown or segmented control labeled clearly (e.g., ‘Sort by distance’), with 8–16px horizontal padding around the control itself so it does not feel cramped. On smaller viewports, the sort-control can stack below the results-summary but retains right or center alignment to maintain visual balance and hierarchy. The overall toolbar height remains modest to avoid competing visually with the hero and cards.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Part of a shared horizontal toolbar with the results-summary; positioned in normal flow, right-aligned within a flex or grid row that adjusts to stack controls on smaller screens.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The results-summary shares a horizontal toolbar row with the sort-control and is anchored to the left side of the centered content container. It appears directly below the filter bars, with approximately 24–32px of vertical space from the bottom of the hours-filter-bar and 16–24px above the first clinic result card. The summary text (e.g., ‘12 clinics near 94110’) is set in a calm, medium-weight type and occupies roughly the left two-thirds of the row on desktop, leaving room for the sort-control on the right. This left-aligned placement supports the user’s F-pattern scan and acts as a subtle section header for the results area. On smaller screens, the summary sits above the sort-control in a vertical stack, maintaining a consistent left alignment and clear breathing room between the two. The toolbar background remains the same as the main content (white), so the summary reads as a gentle informational line rather than a heavy banner.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline within a horizontal toolbar row, left-aligned; flows naturally above the results list, with spacing that clearly demarcates the beginning of the results section.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The results-loading-indicator occupies the same central content region as the clinic-results-list, appearing when a search or filter change is in progress. When active, it is vertically centered within the visible portion of the results area beneath the toolbar, with the indicator (spinner or simple animated icon) and a brief status message stacked centrally. Horizontally, it is centered within the 1100–1200px content container, with ample whitespace (at least 32–48px) on all sides so it feels light and non-intrusive. On desktop, if there are already results on screen, the loading indicator can appear just above the list or as a semi-transparent overlay centered over the cards, dimming them slightly to indicate updating while preserving the spatial structure of the page. It should never extend into the hero or header area, staying visually tied to the results region.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Temporarily overlays or replaces the contents of the results region; centered within the results area and layered above cards when overlaying, but constrained to the main content container.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The clinic-results-list is placed immediately below the results toolbar (results-summary and sort-control), separated by a comfortable 16–24px vertical gap to indicate the start of the list. It occupies the full width of the centered content container (1100–1200px max), with consistent side padding so the cards align neatly with elements above. On desktop, the list is a single vertical column of clinic-result-card components, one per row, to maintain a calm and easily scannable layout; there is a 24–32px vertical gap between consecutive cards to emphasize each as a distinct option. The list extends downward, forming the primary scrollable area of the page, with at least 64–96px of bottom padding before the end of the page so the last card never feels cramped against the viewport edge. On smaller screens, the list maintains its single-column structure but reduces side margins slightly to maximize usable space while preserving adequate whitespace.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard vertically scrolling list in normal flow; defines the main scrollable region of the page beneath the hero and filter areas.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Each clinic-result-card sits within the clinic-results-list as a full-width card aligned to the central content grid. On desktop, the card width matches the list’s inner width (minus standard horizontal padding), creating a rectangular block that spans approximately 80–90% of the viewport width depending on the overall container size, with consistent left and right alignment across all cards. The card uses generous internal padding of 16–24px on all sides and subtle corner rounding, reinforcing the modern, therapeutic aesthetic. Content within the card is arranged with primary information (clinic name, key attributes) on the left and secondary actions (e.g., ‘View details’, distance label) aligned to the right or bottom-right, but always within the same card boundaries. Vertically, cards are separated by 24–32px of space so they read as distinct, tappable units with plenty of breathing room. On smaller screens, the card’s internal layout collapses into a clean vertical stack, maintaining the same outer margins and padding so the card continues to feel spacious and touch-friendly.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Block-level element inside the list; no special behavior beyond responsive internal layout, maintaining fixed alignment and spacing within the column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The empty-results-state appears in place of the clinic-results-list whenever a search returns no matching locations. It occupies the same horizontal bounds as the results list, centered within the 1100–1200px content container beneath the results toolbar. Vertically, it uses generous whitespace: a margin of about 24–32px from the toolbar above and enough height to visually center its content in the available viewport area where possible, without overlapping the hero. Within this area, a simple, friendly illustration or icon can sit above a concise explanatory message and a subtle call to action (e.g., ‘Adjust filters’), all center-aligned. The surrounding padding (at least 32–48px on all sides) ensures that the empty state feels calm and reassuring rather than alarming or cramped. When this state is visible, no clinic-result-card elements are shown, keeping the space visually clean.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Mutually exclusive with the clinic-results-list in the results region; appears in normal flow directly beneath the results toolbar when there are zero results.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The url-state-manager is a non-visual component that resides logically alongside the locations-page-controller, synchronizing the state of the search form, filters, sort, and current results with the browser URL. It does not occupy any visual space and does not alter the physical layout of the page. Instead, it ensures that when users share a link or navigate back and forth, the hero, search form, filters, and results restore to the correct configuration without any visible jumps or reflows beyond normal loading behavior.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-rendering logic layer; no spatial footprint or behavior in the layout, purely functional state synchronization.

name: Location Detail
title: Location Detail
description: Displays detailed information for a specific clinic location, including contact details, map, hours, amenities, services, therapists, and an inline callback request form.

core_features: Load and display detailed clinic information for the locationId provided in the URL using getLocationDetail.
Show clinic contact information including name, full address, phone number, and email.
Display a map section centered on the clinic’s latitude and longitude when available.
Show clinic hours, including an indication of Saturday availability when openSaturday is true.
Display location amenities and indicators for features such as free parking based on hasFreeParking and amenities.
List key services offered at this clinic using servicesOffered from getLocationDetail.
List or summarize therapists associated with this clinic using therapists from getLocationDetail, with navigation to therapists.html for full details.
Provide a prominent request-a-call action that reveals an inline callback request form.
Allow users to submit a callback request for this clinic using submitCallbackRequest with locationId, phoneNumber, preferredCallTime, and an optional message.
Provide navigation back to the locations search results and a link to view other nearby clinics via locations.html, as well as a link to therapists.html with the current locationId.
user_workflows:
View a specific clinic location:
1. User navigates to location_detail.html with a URL parameter locationId.
2. On page initialization, the page reads locationId from the URL.
3. The page calls getLocationDetail(locationId).
4. While waiting, a loading state is shown for all location-dependent components.
5. On success, the page populates the header, map, hours, amenities, services list, and therapists summary from the getLocationDetail response.
6. If getLocationDetail fails (e.g., invalid locationId), an error message is displayed and navigation back to locations.html is offered.

Request a callback from the clinic:
1. User is viewing a location detail that has been loaded via getLocationDetail.
2. User clicks the primary “Request a Call” button.
3. The page scrolls to or expands the inline callback form component and can optionally update the URL to include showCallbackForm=1.
4. User enters a phone number, selects a preferred call time (mapped to one of the preferredCallTime enum values), and optionally types a note.
5. On form submission, client-side validation checks for required fields (phoneNumber and preferredCallTime) and basic phone format.
6. If validation passes, the page calls submitCallbackRequest with { locationId (from URL), phoneNumber, preferredCallTime, message }.
7. While awaiting the response, the form shows a submitting state and disables inputs.
8. On success (response.success is true), the form switches to a confirmation state displaying response.message and response.nextStepsText, and the callbackRequestId can be stored in in-memory state; optionally, the URL can be updated with callbackSuccess=1.
9. On error (response.success is false or network failure), the component shows an error message and allows resubmission.

Navigate back to locations search:
1. User clicks the “Back to Locations Search” link or button.
2. The browser navigates to locations.html (with no required additional parameters, or with any existing search context preserved if present from the referring URL).
3. locations.html handles its own loading and filtering logic.

View therapists at this clinic:
1. User scrolls to the therapists summary section populated from getLocationDetail.therapists.
2. User clicks a link or button labeled to view therapists at this clinic.
3. The browser navigates to therapists.html with a URL parameter locationId set to the current locationId.
4. therapists.html uses its own interfaces and logic to display the therapist directory filtered to this location.

Find other nearby clinics from this location:
1. User is on location_detail.html with a loaded location (including latitude and longitude when available).
2. User clicks a link labeled to view other nearby clinics.
3. The browser navigates to locations.html with URL parameters that reference the current location, such as nearLocationId set to the current locationId (and optionally lat/long parameters if the other page supports them).
4. locations.html interprets these parameters to show nearby clinics.
interactions: On page initialization, read locationId from the URL and trigger getLocationDetail; if locationId is missing or empty, show an error state and a navigation option to locations.html.
Clicking the “Back to Locations Search” control triggers a navigation to locations.html without calling any additional API.
Clicking the “View therapists at this clinic” control triggers navigation to therapists.html?locationId=<current-locationId>.
Clicking the “View other nearby clinics” control triggers navigation to locations.html with a nearLocationId parameter set to the current locationId.
Clicking the primary callback CTA button toggles the visibility of the callback form component and may update the URL to include or remove showCallbackForm=1.
Typing into the phone number input in the callback form updates the local form state for phoneNumber and clears any previous validation errors tied to that field.

Changing the preferred call time selection updates the local form state for preferredCallTime; the selection options map directly to the CallbackRequest.preferred_call_time enum values (morning_9_12, afternoon_12_4, evening_4_7, anytime).
Typing into the optional note field in the callback form updates the local form state for message.

Submitting the callback form triggers client-side validation; on success, it calls submitCallbackRequest with locationId (from URL), phoneNumber, preferredCallTime, and message; on failure, it shows inline validation errors.

Receiving a successful response from submitCallbackRequest updates the form state to a confirmation mode and displays response.message and response.nextStepsText; the callbackRequestId from the response can be stored in component state for display or analytics.
Receiving an error response or network failure from submitCallbackRequest shows an error message, keeps the form data intact, and re-enables the submit button.

If the map coordinates (latitude and longitude) are present, the map component initializes or updates the embedded map; if coordinates are absent, the component displays a location-unavailable state without calling additional interfaces.
page_functionality.state_logic:
The primary page state is determined by the URL parameter locationId. On initial load and on any change to locationId in the URL, the page calls getLocationDetail(locationId) and refreshes all dependent components (header, map, hours, amenities, services list, and therapists summary). If locationId is missing or invalid, the page shows an error state and prompts the user to go back to locations.html.

Additional UI state can be synchronized with URL parameters to support deep linking:
- showCallbackForm: When present and truthy in the query string (e.g., location_detail.html?locationId=...&showCallbackForm=1), the callback form component initializes in an expanded/visible state, and the page may auto-scroll to it after location data loads.
- callbackSuccess: After a successful call to submitCallbackRequest, the page may append callbackSuccess=1 to the URL so that reloading the page can show a generic "request received" acknowledgment instead of an empty form. Because there is no interface on this page to fetch a specific CallbackRequest, detailed server-side confirmation text (like response.nextStepsText) is only available in the current session state, not after a full reload.

All navigations to other pages pass state exclusively via URL parameters: navigating to therapists.html includes locationId, and navigating to locations.html may include nearLocationId or other location-aware parameters. No localStorage or other client-side persistence is used for core logic on this page.

components.functionality: Reads the locationId from the URL, calls getLocationDetail(locationId), manages loading and error states, and provides the resulting location data to child components.
Displays the clinic’s name and key contact details (address, phone number, email) using the data returned by getLocationDetail and provides top-level navigation controls.
Uses latitude and longitude from getLocationDetail to initialize and update a map centered on the clinic; if coordinates are unavailable, shows a contextual fallback message.
Shows the clinic’s hours summary, indicates whether the clinic is open on Saturdays, and lists amenities such as free parking and other amenity flags from the location data.

Displays the list of services offered at this clinic using the servicesOffered array from getLocationDetail, optionally linking each service to related information pages (if those routes exist in the broader application).
Lists or summarizes therapists associated with this clinic from getLocationDetail.therapists and includes a control to navigate to therapists.html filtered by the current locationId.

Provides navigation to locations.html configured to search for nearby clinics based on the current location’s identifier (and optionally its coordinates) by appending appropriate query parameters such as nearLocationId.
Provides a prominent button or link to initiate the callback request flow; toggles the visibility of the callback form and can update the URL to set or remove showCallbackForm=1.

Collects the user’s phoneNumber, preferredCallTime (mapped to the enum values morning_9_12, afternoon_12_4, evening_4_7, anytime), and an optional message, validates the input, and submits a callback request via submitCallbackRequest using the current locationId.

Displays submission status and confirmation for the callback request. On successful submitCallbackRequest, it shows the server’s response.message and response.nextStepsText; it can also interpret URL flags like callbackSuccess=1 to show a generic success message on reload.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column desktop grid with a centered max-width content container (~1100–1200px) inside full-width color bands.
layout.layout_pattern: Use a linear, top-to-bottom flow inside a centered max-width container (~1100–1200px). Start with a full-width, colored location header band that visually echoes the hero from the mockup (solid slate-blue, centered title). Below this, place a two-column row for map and hours/amenities, followed by full-width stacked sections for services, therapists, and finally a dedicated callback band containing the CTA, form, and status. The nearby-locations link sits near the bottom as a light navigational escape.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Use a linear, top-to-bottom flow inside a centered max-width container (~1100–1200px). Start with a full-width, colored location header band that visually echoes the hero from the mockup (solid slate-blue, centered title). Below this, place a two-column row for map and hours/amenities, followed by full-width stacked sections for services, therapists, and finally a dedicated callback band containing the CTA, form, and status. The nearby-locations link sits near the bottom as a light navigational escape.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The location header occupies the visual role of a hero for this page, taking roughly 35–45% of the initial viewport height with generous top and bottom padding (around 64–96px). The map + hours row beneath it uses most of the remaining initial viewport height. Subsequent sections (services, therapists, callback) each form their own vertical bands with 64–96px separation. Within the grid, the map takes about 2/3 width and the hours/amenities about 1/3, reflecting the header’s 1/3 logo and 2/3 nav relationship.
layout_strategies.content_density: Maintain low content density with clear breathing room: large vertical gaps between sections (64–96px), generous internal padding in cards and panels (16–24px), and limited items per visual cluster. Each section focuses on one main task: understanding the location, finding it (map + hours), seeing what’s offered (services, therapists), then requesting contact (callback). Lists (services, therapists) are structured but not crowded, favoring 2–3 columns with ample whitespace.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Mirroring the mockup, the user’s eye lands first on the centered title in the colored location header. From there, the flow moves downward into the main content: first to the left-aligned map (primary focal point) and then across to the right-aligned hours/amenities, echoing the header’s subtle F-pattern. The flow then continues straight down through the services and therapists sections before settling on the callback area as the final strong focal block. The nearby-locations link provides a gentle exit at the end of the scroll.

layout.overall_description: The page presents as a calm, structured interior detail view with a strong, centered location header at the top, set in a full-width slate-blue band similar to the original hero. Inside this band, the location name is centered both horizontally and vertically, surrounded by generous empty space and a subtle organic motif, making it the clear focal point upon arrival. Below this, the content sits on a clean white background within a centered max-width container aligned to a 12‑column grid. The first content row pairs a wide map on the left with a narrower hours and amenities card on the right, forming a stable, two-column block that fills much of the first scroll. Subsequent sections stack vertically with consistent spacing: a full-width services list, then a full-width therapists summary, each clearly separated by breathing room and using simple, readable typography. Near the bottom, a softly tinted band spans the width of the container, housing a centered callback request CTA and form, echoing the hero’s centered composition but at a smaller scale. A nearby-locations link at the bottom provides a quiet navigation option without competing visually with the primary content. Overall the layout feels orderly, spacious, and reassuring, guiding the user from understanding where they are, to what is offered, to how to get in touch.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The data loader appears as a semi-transparent overlay centered over the entire location detail content area, including the location header and all subsequent sections. It dims the underlying page with a soft white or slate-tinted veil, while a simple loading indicator (spinner or progress icon) is centered both horizontally and vertically in the viewport. When active, it visually sits above all other components, but once data is loaded it disappears entirely, revealing the structured vertical layout beneath.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Behaves as a fixed overlay relative to the viewport, covering the full width and height of the visible area while active. It does not affect the underlying document flow; it simply layers on top with a high z-index and is removed when loading completes.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Positioned at the top of the location detail page content, immediately below any global site header, the location header occupies a full-width horizontal band with a slate-blue background that visually echoes the hero from the mockup. Within this band, a centered max-width container (~1100–1200px) holds the content. The location name is centered horizontally and vertically within the band, typically on one or two lines, using large, calm typography. It may sit inside or over an organic ink-cloud-like shape aligned to the horizontal center, mirroring the mockup’s central focal graphic. Below the main title, a smaller, centered subtitle line (e.g., city, address, or clinic type) appears, separated by 16–24px. Vertical padding above and below the text is generous—roughly 64–96px—so the header fills about the upper third to half of the initial viewport, with abundant negative space around the title.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block-level section that spans 100% of the viewport width. It scrolls with the page (not sticky), forming the visual entry point for the location detail content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Directly below the location header, after a vertical gap of about 64px, the map panel appears as the left column of the first content row within the white background area. Inside the centered max-width container, the map spans approximately 7–8 of the 12 grid columns (roughly 60–65% of the container width), aligned to the left. It presents as a rectangular card or panel with subtle rounding and a faint shadow or border, visually anchored to the top of the row. Its height is substantial (around 360–480px) to make the map clearly legible and to visually balance the header above. There is comfortable internal padding above any title/label at the top of the card, but the map view itself stretches close to the panel edges for maximum usable area. A 24–32px gutter separates it from the hours/amenities panel on the right.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Behaves as a standard grid-aligned block within the main container. On narrower viewports, it stacks above the hours/amenities panel, expanding to nearly the full container width while maintaining top alignment and vertical spacing consistent with other sections.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: In the same horizontal row as the map, the hours and amenities panel sits to the right, aligned to the top edge of the map panel. It occupies the remaining 4–5 columns of the 12-column grid (roughly 35–40% of the container width). This panel is a vertically oriented card with rounded corners and a subtle border or shadow, matching the visual style of the map card. It is slightly narrower but similar in height to the map panel, creating a balanced side-by-side composition. Inside, the clinic hours appear at the top as a small heading followed by a tidy list, with amenities listed below, each separated by 8–16px of vertical space. Generous internal padding (around 24px on all sides) keeps the content airy and easy to scan. The overall effect is that of a calm, information-dense but visually light summary block complementing the map.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard grid-aligned card that sits to the right of the map on desktop. On tablet and mobile, it flows under the map panel, maintaining full-width alignment with 32–48px vertical spacing, preserving hierarchy but prioritizing linear readability.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: After the map and hours/amenities row, a vertical gap of around 64–80px introduces the services list as the next full-width section within the main container. It spans the entire 12-column width (within the centered max-width) and reads as its own content band on the white background. A section heading (e.g., “Services at this location”) is left-aligned at the top, with ample space (24–32px) below before the services entries begin. The individual services may be arranged in 2–3 columns of simple text or small cards, depending on density, but each item has generous vertical spacing (at least 8–12px) to maintain a calm feel. The overall section height is moderate to tall, but the white space and consistent alignment prevent it from feeling crowded.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block-level section that stacks beneath the map/hours section. It follows the 12-column grid for any internal columns, and on smaller screens the services naturally collapse into a single-column list while retaining the same top and bottom spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Below the services list, separated by another 64–80px of vertical space, the therapists summary occupies a full-width section within the same centered container. A clear heading at the top (e.g., “Our therapists at this location”) anchors the section. Under the heading, therapist entries are presented as individual cards or rows, each with sufficient padding (16–24px) and spacing (16–24px between items) to keep the layout open and approachable. On desktop, these cards may be arranged in a 2–3 column grid across the 12 columns, maintaining consistent card widths and aligning card tops to create a stable visual rhythm. The section can grow vertically as needed, but gutters and margins ensure that it never feels like a wall of content.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block that stacks below the services list. Internally, it uses the 12-column grid for therapist cards; on narrow screens it collapses to a single-column list of therapist cards while keeping the same generous section padding and spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The nearby locations link appears near the bottom of the main content, positioned just below the therapists summary or near the bottom of the callback section (depending on where it best fits in the product). It is a single-line text link with subtle styling, placed inside the max-width container. On desktop, it is right-aligned or slightly offset to the right of center, sitting with about 32–48px of space above it to visually separate it from the section above, and 48–64px below it before the page footer. Its light visual weight and compact size make it feel like a quiet navigational option rather than a competing call-to-action.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard inline/block-level navigation element that participates in the normal vertical flow. It is not sticky or floating; it simply appears as a concluding link at the bottom of the content stack.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The callback request CTA is the entry point to a dedicated contact section near the bottom of the page, appearing after the therapists summary with a vertical gap of about 64–80px. It is placed within a subtle, full-width tinted band (e.g., a very light version of the hero slate-blue) that stretches edge-to-edge of the viewport, while the content itself remains centered within the max-width container. At the top of this band, a brief heading or sentence invites users to request a callback. Directly beneath this text, centered horizontally, the primary CTA button stands out with clear contrast. The button has generous padding (at least 16px vertically and 24–32px horizontally) and sits with 16–24px of space below it before the form begins, mirroring the centered focal treatment of the hero but at a smaller scale.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard inline/block element within the callback band, centered within the container. It scrolls naturally with the page and stays visually attached to the callback form and status components directly below.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The callback request form sits directly below the CTA button within the same tinted band, forming the core of the callback section. It is centered horizontally in the container and constrained to a comfortable width of about 50–60% of the content area (roughly 6–7 of 12 columns), so it feels approachable rather than expansive. The form fields are arranged in a simple vertical stack with consistent spacing (16–24px between fields), and labels are clearly separated from inputs. There is generous padding at the top and bottom of the entire callback band (around 48–64px), providing breathing room around the form. The submit button aligns with the form content, typically left-aligned within the form’s column but visually tied to the center via the form’s overall alignment.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block-level form that sits in the vertical flow within the tinted callback band. On smaller screens, it expands to nearly full width while maintaining the same centered alignment and vertical spacing between elements.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The callback request status panel appears immediately below the form within the callback band, sharing the same centered alignment and width. When active, it shows as a compact but clearly defined message area—either a full-width strip within the form column or a small card—with subtle background tint and rounded corners to differentiate it from both the form and the band background. It is separated from the form by about 16–24px of vertical space. When inactive, this area may either collapse or reserve a minimal amount of space to prevent distracting layout shifts. Its visual tone remains calm and supportive, using clear, concise text.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block-level element that appears directly beneath the form within the callback band. It participates in normal document flow, expanding or collapsing based on the presence of a status message, but it does not overlay or float.

name: Weekly Planner
title: Weekly Planner
description: Create, view, edit, and save a child’s weekly therapy plan by adding sessions across the week, and manage previously saved plans.

core_features: Initialize the planner based on the optional URL parameter weeklyPlanId and load the corresponding weekly plan draft using plannerLoadSavedPlan.
Start a new empty weekly plan draft using plannerStartNewPlan when the user chooses to create a new plan.
Retrieve and keep the current plan draft sessions in sync for display in the Week View using plannerGetCurrentPlan.
Add new therapy sessions to the current weekly plan draft via plannerAddSessionToCurrentPlan using user-selected service, child name, day of week, start time, duration, and notes.
Edit existing sessions in the current weekly plan draft using plannerUpdateSessionInCurrentPlan.
Remove sessions from the current weekly plan draft using plannerRemoveSessionFromCurrentPlan.
Save the current weekly plan draft with a user-defined plan name using plannerSaveCurrentPlan, updating saved plans state without localStorage.
List saved weekly plans in a sidebar using plannerListSavedPlans and allow the user to open one by navigating to planner.html with the corresponding weeklyPlanId URL parameter.
Display sessions in a structured Week View and a Weekly Summary view based on the sessions array returned by planner* interfaces.
Provide navigation to a services information page (services.html) via a clear in-page link.
user_workflows:
Workflow 1: Open planner with or without a specific saved plan
1) User navigates to planner.html.
2) The page-init controller parses the URL for weeklyPlanId.
3) If weeklyPlanId is present, the page calls plannerLoadSavedPlan(weeklyPlanId).
4) On success, the page stores planDraftId, planName, childName, and sessions in in-memory state and initializes the Week View and Summary.
5) If weeklyPlanId is not present, the page does not load a plan by default and shows a state prompting the user to click “Create New Weekly Plan”.
6) Independently of weeklyPlanId, the page calls plannerListSavedPlans to populate the saved plans sidebar.

Workflow 2: Start a new weekly plan draft
1) User clicks the “Create New Weekly Plan” button in the toolbar.
2) The page calls plannerStartNewPlan().
3) On success, the page stores the returned planDraftId and clears any current draft sessions state.
4) The Week View switches to an empty state for the new draft, and the user can start adding sessions.
5) If there were unsaved changes in a previous draft, the UI can optionally show a confirmation dialog before discarding those in-memory changes (no additional API calls).

Workflow 3: Add a new therapy session to the plan
1) User clicks an “Add Session” button.
2) A session form opens, allowing the user to select a service (serviceId), enter child name, choose dayOfWeek, specify startTime, durationMinutes, and optional notes.
3) On submit, the page calls plannerAddSessionToCurrentPlan(planDraftId, serviceId, childName, dayOfWeek, startTime, durationMinutes, notes).
4) On success, the interface returns an updated sessions array; the page replaces its current sessions state with this array.
5) The Week View and Weekly Summary components re-render based on the updated sessions.
6) The UI flags that there are unsaved changes in this draft until the user explicitly saves.

Workflow 4: Edit an existing session in the plan
1) In the Week View, the user selects a session and clicks an “Edit” control.
2) The session form opens prefilled with that session’s existing values (serviceId, childName, dayOfWeek, startTime, durationMinutes, notes).
3) User updates one or more fields and submits the form.
4) The page calls plannerUpdateSessionInCurrentPlan(planDraftId, sessionId, serviceId, dayOfWeek, startTime, durationMinutes, notes).
5) On success, the returned sessions array is used to refresh the local sessions state.
6) The Week View and Weekly Summary update immediately to reflect the changes, and the draft is marked as having unsaved changes.

Workflow 5: Delete a session from the plan
1) In the Week View, the user clicks a “Delete” or “Remove” control on a specific session.
2) A confirmation prompt appears (handled entirely in the UI layer).
3) If confirmed, the page calls plannerRemoveSessionFromCurrentPlan(planDraftId, sessionId).
4) On success, the page updates its sessions state with the returned sessions array.
5) The Week View and Weekly Summary re-render without the removed session, and the draft is marked as having unsaved changes.

Workflow 6: Save the current weekly plan
1) User clicks the “Save Plan” button in the toolbar.
2) A save modal or inline form appears, prompting the user to enter or update the plan name (planName).
3) On submit, the page calls plannerSaveCurrentPlan(planDraftId, planName).
4) On success, the page shows a confirmation message and records the returned weeklyPlanId, planName, childName, and totalSessions in in-memory state.
5) The page then refreshes the saved plans sidebar by calling plannerListSavedPlans.
6) Unsaved-changes indicators are cleared because the draft now corresponds to the latest saved version.

Workflow 7: Open a saved weekly plan from the sidebar
1) The saved plans sidebar shows a list of plans from plannerListSavedPlans.
2) User identifies a plan and clicks its “Open” control.
3) The page navigates to planner.html with the selected plan’s weeklyPlanId added as a URL parameter (planner.html?weeklyPlanId=...).
4) On load, the init controller detects weeklyPlanId and calls plannerLoadSavedPlan(weeklyPlanId).
5) On success, the loaded plan’s planDraftId, planName, childName, and sessions become the current draft; the Week View and Summary are updated accordingly.
6) The active plan in the saved plans sidebar can be highlighted by comparing each listed weeklyPlanId with the one in the current URL.

Workflow 8: View Week View and Weekly Summary
1) Whenever sessions state changes (via plannerLoadSavedPlan, plannerGetCurrentPlan, plannerAddSessionToCurrentPlan, plannerUpdateSessionInCurrentPlan, or plannerRemoveSessionFromCurrentPlan), the page updates a shared sessions state array.
2) The Week View component presents sessions grouped by dayOfWeek and ordered by startTime using this array.
3) The Weekly Summary component uses the same array to compute summary information (such as counts per day or per service) entirely on the client side without additional APIs.
4) Any subsequent changes to sessions automatically propagate to both views through reactive data binding.

Workflow 9: Navigate to services information
1) User clicks a “Learn more about our services” link available within the planner UI.
2) The browser navigates to services.html without additional URL parameters.
3) The planner page does not call any interfaces for this navigation; it performs a simple link navigation.
interactions: On page load, parse the URL for weeklyPlanId and call plannerLoadSavedPlan if present; also call plannerListSavedPlans to populate the saved plans sidebar.
Clicking the “Create New Weekly Plan” button triggers plannerStartNewPlan and initializes a new empty draft using the returned planDraftId.
Clicking the “Add Session” button opens a session form for entering serviceId, childName, dayOfWeek, startTime, durationMinutes, and notes; submitting the form calls plannerAddSessionToCurrentPlan.
Clicking an “Edit” control on a session in the Week View opens the session form prefilled with that session’s data; submitting the form calls plannerUpdateSessionInCurrentPlan.
Clicking a “Delete” control on a session opens a confirmation; confirming calls plannerRemoveSessionFromCurrentPlan, and on success the session disappears from the Week View and Summary.
Clicking the “Save Plan” button opens a plan name entry form; submitting the form calls plannerSaveCurrentPlan and then refreshes the saved plans list with plannerListSavedPlans.
Clicking an “Open” control on a saved plan in the sidebar navigates the browser to planner.html?weeklyPlanId=<selected weekly plan id>, causing the page to reinitialize for that plan.
Clicking the “Learn more about our services” link navigates to services.html.

When planDraftId is available (after starting or loading a plan), the Week View component can call plannerGetCurrentPlan(planDraftId) on demand (e.g., initial draft load or manual refresh), and also listen to updates from add/update/remove operations to keep sessions current.
Closing modals or banners (for save success, error messages, or unsaved-change warnings) is handled via local UI state only and does not involve any planner interfaces.
page_functionality.state_logic: The primary cross-navigation state is carried via the URL parameter weeklyPlanId. When planner.html is loaded, the page inspects window.location and, if weeklyPlanId is present, calls plannerLoadSavedPlan(weeklyPlanId). The response provides a planDraftId, planName, childName, and sessions, which become the active in-memory draft state for this page view. If weeklyPlanId is absent, the page starts with no active draft until the user clicks “Create New Weekly Plan”, which calls plannerStartNewPlan and returns a planDraftId used for all subsequent planner* calls. The planDraftId itself is maintained in page runtime state (e.g., in a client-side store or component state) and is not persisted in the URL, ensuring that all plannerAddSessionToCurrentPlan, plannerUpdateSessionInCurrentPlan, plannerRemoveSessionFromCurrentPlan, plannerGetCurrentPlan, and plannerSaveCurrentPlan calls always pass the current planDraftId. Saved plans are discovered via plannerListSavedPlans when the page loads or after a successful save, and each saved plan can be reopened by navigating to planner.html?weeklyPlanId=<id>, which fully reinitializes the page state for that plan. No localStorage or other persistent client-side storage is used; all persistent state derives from the WeeklyPlan and WeeklyPlanSession data via the planner interfaces and the weeklyPlanId URL parameter, while transient UI state (such as open modals, form inputs, and unsaved-change flags) is maintained only in memory during the current page session.

components.functionality: Controls initial page setup: reads the weeklyPlanId URL parameter, loads a saved plan into a new draft when present, initializes the current plan draft state, and fetches the list of saved plans.
event_handlers: onPageLoad: parse URL for weeklyPlanId and decide whether to call plannerLoadSavedPlan or leave the page in 'no active draft' state.
onPageLoad: call plannerListSavedPlans() and store the returned array for the saved-plans sidebar.
onWeeklyPlanIdParamChange: when navigation to a new weeklyPlanId occurs (e.g., from the sidebar), re-run plannerLoadSavedPlan with the new id and reset current draft state.

components.functionality: Provides primary controls for managing the current plan draft: shows the page title, displays current plan name and child summary, offers buttons to create a new weekly plan, save the current plan, and navigate to the services information page.
event_handlers: onClickCreateNewPlan: call plannerStartNewPlan(), set the returned planDraftId as the current draft, clear current sessions state, and mark the draft as unsaved.
onClickSavePlan: open the plan-save-modal component with the current planDraftId and any existing planName to allow the user to confirm or edit the name before saving.
onClickLearnMoreServices: navigate the browser to services.html without additional parameters.

components.functionality: Collects or updates the plan name for the current draft and persists it using plannerSaveCurrentPlan. After saving, it updates the active plan information and refreshes the saved plans list.
event_handlers: onOpen: initialize the form with the current planName (if any) and associate it with the active planDraftId.

onSubmitSavePlan: call plannerSaveCurrentPlan(planDraftId, planName) and, on success, update the in-memory representation of the current plan (including weeklyPlanId, planName, childName, totalSessions) and close the modal.
onSubmitSavePlanSuccess: call plannerListSavedPlans() to refresh the saved plans sidebar with the latest plans.
onSubmitSavePlanError: display an error message in the plan-status-banner component and keep the modal open for correction.
onCancel: close the modal without calling any planner interface.

components.functionality: Handles both adding and editing of individual therapy sessions within the current weekly plan draft by collecting service, child name, day of week, start time, duration, and notes, and calling the appropriate planner interface.
event_handlers: onOpenAddMode: clear form fields and associate the form with the current planDraftId to prepare for a new session; set internal mode='add'.
onOpenEditMode: populate form fields with the selected session’s data (sessionId, serviceId, childName, dayOfWeek, startTime, durationMinutes, notes) and set mode='edit'.

onSubmitAddSession: when mode='add', call plannerAddSessionToCurrentPlan(planDraftId, serviceId, childName, dayOfWeek, startTime, durationMinutes, notes); on success, update the shared sessions state with the returned sessions array, close the modal, and mark the current draft as having unsaved changes.

onSubmitEditSession: when mode='edit', call plannerUpdateSessionInCurrentPlan(planDraftId, sessionId, serviceId, dayOfWeek, startTime, durationMinutes, notes); on success, update the shared sessions state with the returned sessions array, close the modal, and mark the current draft as having unsaved changes.
onSubmitError: present validation or API error messages via plan-status-banner and keep the form open.
onCancel: close the modal without calling any planner interface.

components.functionality: Displays all sessions in the current weekly plan draft in a structured Week View, grouped by day of week and ordered by start time, and provides controls to edit or delete individual sessions.
event_handlers: onPlanDraftReady: when a planDraftId becomes available (after plannerStartNewPlan or plannerLoadSavedPlan), call plannerGetCurrentPlan(planDraftId) to retrieve the current sessions (if not already provided by the load API) and initialize the grid.

onSessionsStateChange: re-render the grid whenever the shared sessions array is updated by plannerLoadSavedPlan, plannerAddSessionToCurrentPlan, plannerUpdateSessionInCurrentPlan, or plannerRemoveSessionFromCurrentPlan.
onClickAddSession: open session-form-modal in add mode for the selected day of week (if the UI provides day-specific add controls).
onClickEditSession: when the user selects a session, open session-form-modal in edit mode with that session’s data.

onClickDeleteSession: prompt the user for confirmation, then call plannerRemoveSessionFromCurrentPlan(planDraftId, sessionId); on success, replace the shared sessions array with the returned sessions and mark the draft as having unsaved changes.
onManualRefresh: optionally allow a manual refresh button that calls plannerGetCurrentPlan(planDraftId) to resync sessions if needed.

components.functionality: Provides an at-a-glance summary of the current weekly plan draft, such as total number of sessions and distribution by day or service, computed entirely from the sessions array without additional API calls.
event_handlers: onSessionsStateChange: recompute summary metrics whenever the shared sessions array changes due to plannerLoadSavedPlan, plannerGetCurrentPlan, plannerAddSessionToCurrentPlan, plannerUpdateSessionInCurrentPlan, or plannerRemoveSessionFromCurrentPlan.
onPlanSaved: optionally update summary display using totalSessions returned by plannerSaveCurrentPlan for consistency checks (no extra calls).

components.functionality: Lists all saved weekly plans for the current user and allows the user to open a plan, which triggers navigation to planner.html with the appropriate weeklyPlanId parameter.
event_handlers: onInit: call plannerListSavedPlans() to retrieve the list of saved plans and render them with metadata such as name, childName, createdAt, updatedAt, and totalSessions.
onAfterPlanSaved: when plannerSaveCurrentPlan succeeds, call plannerListSavedPlans() again to refresh the sidebar list.
onClickOpenPlan: when a user selects a saved plan, navigate to planner.html?weeklyPlanId=<selected weeklyPlanId>, causing a full page reload and reinitialization through plannerLoadSavedPlan.
onHighlightCurrentPlan: compare each listed weeklyPlanId with the weeklyPlanId in the current URL to visually distinguish the active plan in the sidebar.

components.functionality: Displays global feedback messages related to planner actions, such as save success, error messages, and unsaved changes indicators, based on responses from planner interfaces.
event_handlers: onApiCallSuccess: show contextual success messages when planner interfaces return success=true (e.g., plan saved, session added/updated/removed, plan loaded).
onApiCallError: show error details when any planner interface returns success=false or fails, and provide guidance to retry the action.
onUnsavedChangesDetected: when the draft state diverges from the last saved state (after add/update/remove without a subsequent save), show an unsaved-changes notice to the user.
onDismissMessage: allow the user to close or dismiss current banners without triggering any planner interface calls.

components.functionality: Provides a simple, clearly labeled link inside the planner page that navigates users to more information about available services.
event_handlers: onClickNavigateToServices: navigate the browser to services.html without parameters.

layout.grid_system: Implicit 12-column desktop grid with a centered max-width content container (~1100–1200px) inside full-width color bands.
layout.layout_pattern: The page follows the same top-down, linear structure as the mockup: a global white header (logo left, nav right), then a full-width slate-blue hero band with a centered ink-cloud and the 'Weekly Planner' title, and below that the main planner workspace. Inside the workspace, content uses a centered max-width container on a white background. The planner tools appear first (toolbar and status banner), followed by a two-column main area: the weekly grid as the central focal element on the left, and supporting information (summary and saved plans) stacked on the right. A simple, centered navigation link to services sits at the bottom as a gentle exit point.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page follows the same top-down, linear structure as the mockup: a global white header (logo left, nav right), then a full-width slate-blue hero band with a centered ink-cloud and the 'Weekly Planner' title, and below that the main planner workspace. Inside the workspace, content uses a centered max-width container on a white background. The planner tools appear first (toolbar and status banner), followed by a two-column main area: the weekly grid as the central focal element on the left, and supporting information (summary and saved plans) stacked on the right. A simple, centered navigation link to services sits at the bottom as a gentle exit point.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The header consumes the top ~15% of the viewport height, with the hero occupying ~70–80% of the initial viewport height, giving a large calm introduction. The main planner container below the hero spans a max-width of ~1100–1200px and is centered within the page. Within this container, the weekly grid uses roughly two-thirds of the horizontal space (about 8/12 columns), while the right-side column (summary + saved plans) uses about one-third (4/12 columns). Vertical spacing between major bands (hero → planner tools → grid → footer link) follows the generous 64–96px rhythm to maintain the airy feel.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content density is intentionally low: few primary elements per horizontal band and ample whitespace around each. The hero features only the ink-cloud and page title. The planner tools area holds a single toolbar row plus a slim status banner, both with clear breathing room above and below. The main planner area has just two primary blocks in a two-column layout, with generous internal padding and clear separation. The saved plans list is visually restrained with comfortable line spacing, preventing crowding even when many items are present. Modals are centered with generous padding and minimal surrounding chrome to avoid visual overload.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The user’s eye begins at the logo in the top-left, moves right across the global navigation, then drops into the hero’s dramatic centered ink-cloud with the 'Weekly Planner' title. From there, the eye flows down to the planner-header-toolbar, which anchors the functional workspace. The plan-status-banner, when visible, subtly catches attention just below the toolbar. The gaze then moves slightly down and left to the dominant weekly grid, which is the main working area. After exploring the grid, the user naturally glances to the right-hand column for summary feedback and saved plans. Finally, as the user reaches the bottom of the workspace, the centered services-link-block provides a clear, calm next-step navigation focal point.

layout.overall_description: The Weekly Planner page sits within the same calm, modern shell as the rest of the site. At the very top, a white horizontal header contains the logo on the left and the main navigation on the right, spanning the full width of the page. Directly beneath, a full-width slate-blue hero band fills most of the initial viewport. In its center, an organic dark ink-cloud shape floats, with the 'Weekly Planner' title set in two balanced lines of clean, simple type, all centered both horizontally and vertically within the band. Below this hero, the background returns to white and a centered content container (~1120px wide) begins. First within this container is a single-row planner toolbar stretching across most of the width, followed by a slim status banner that, when present, creates a gentle, full-width stripe of information. Further down, the main planning workspace unfolds into a two-column layout: on the left, the weekly grid occupies about two-thirds of the width, with generous padding and clear day columns; on the right, a narrower column stacks a compact summary panel above a taller saved-plans panel. There is plenty of white space between and around these blocks, emphasizing a calm, therapeutic tone and preventing any sense of clutter. As the content tapers off, a wide band of white space leads to a final centered services link at the bottom, acting as a quiet, all-caps-style call to explore other therapy services. When modals appear (for saving plans or editing sessions), they float centered over a softened backdrop, echoing the hero’s central focal-point behavior and allowing users to focus on one task at a time.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The planner-page-init-controller is effectively invisible in the visual layout. It sits logically at the very top of the page’s planner-specific DOM structure, just below the global hero section, but it does not occupy any visible space or alter the spacing between other components. In the visual stack, it behaves like a zero-height element: no background, no border, and no discernible footprint. All other visible components (toolbar, banner, grid, panels, link block) are arranged as if this controller were not present.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual, static in the document flow with zero height and no impact on layout measurements.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The planner-header-toolbar is the first visible planner-specific component beneath the hero band. It sits inside the centered max-width content container, starting approximately 64–80px below the bottom edge of the hero, which preserves the generous gap seen between header and hero in the mockup. The toolbar is a single horizontal strip aligned with the full width of the content container (roughly 100% of the 1100–1200px width, minus standard side padding of 24–32px). Vertically, it is about 56–72px tall, with comfortable internal padding of 16–24px. On larger screens, its content (e.g., current plan name on the left, week selector and primary actions on the right) follows the same left-to-right visual rhythm as the main site header: a key label anchoring the left and action controls clustered toward the right. It has a white background and may sit on a very light subtle divider line above and below to separate it from the hero and the following banner, while still feeling spacious and uncluttered.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block-level element within the main content container. On desktop, it can become sticky once the user scrolls past the hero, pinning to the top of the viewport within the white content area to keep key actions available without disrupting the calm layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The plan-save-modal appears as a centered overlay on top of the entire page, echoing the hero’s central focal-point layout. When triggered, it darkens the background content with a soft, semi-transparent slate-blue or charcoal veil that extends edge-to-edge. The modal itself is positioned in the exact center of the viewport (both horizontally and vertically), with a width of about 480–600px on desktop (roughly 40–50% of the content width) and generous internal padding of 24–32px on all sides to maintain the airy, therapeutic feel. Its height adjusts to content but generally occupies no more than 60% of the viewport height, leaving comfortable space above and below. Inside, the title is centered at the top or aligned left with strong hierarchy, followed by neatly spaced fields and action buttons aligned along the bottom edge. The modal corners are softly rounded, and the overall composition maintains large margins between content sections, mirroring the low information density of the main page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed-position overlay anchored to the center of the viewport, floating above all other components with a full-screen dimmed backdrop. It scrolls internally if content exceeds viewport height, while the underlying page remains static.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The session-form-modal shares the same basic overlay behavior as the plan-save-modal but is slightly larger to accommodate more interactive form fields. When opened, it appears in the center of the viewport, over a semi-transparent darkened backdrop. On desktop, the modal width is about 640–720px (approximately 55–65% of the content container width), with 24–32px of padding and softly rounded corners. Its height can expand but generally stays within 70–80% of the viewport, maintaining visible whitespace above and below the modal. The form layout inside follows a clear top-to-bottom flow with distinct vertical spacing (16–24px) between field groups, avoiding clutter. The primary action buttons are grouped at the bottom right within the modal, with adequate padding and separation so they feel approachable and not crowded. The overall look is clean and calm, acting as a focused, central work surface separate from the underlying weekly grid.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed-position, centered overlay that covers the entire page with a dimmed backdrop. The modal content area can scroll internally if necessary, but the rest of the page remains stationary until the modal is dismissed.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The current-plan-week-view is the main focal area of the planner workspace and sits below the toolbar and plan-status-banner within the centered content container. After a vertical gap of approximately 48–64px below the banner (or toolbar, if no banner is present), the weekly view begins. On desktop, it occupies the left two-thirds of the content container, roughly spanning 8 out of 12 grid columns, starting flush with the container’s left padding. Its width might be around 720–800px within a 1120px container. The week view itself is a horizontal grid of columns (e.g., one per day), with a gentle light background, clear vertical separators, and generous internal padding (16–24px per day cell) so each session block has room to breathe. There is comfortable top and bottom padding around the entire grid (24–32px) to avoid feeling boxed in. Above the grid, a subtle sub-header row can show weekday labels aligned horizontally, mirroring the simple horizontal navigation in the site header but with minimal decoration. Overall, the week view appears as a large, calm, central board that the eye naturally gravitates toward after scanning the toolbar.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block anchored in the left portion of a responsive two-column layout. On narrower screens, it reflows to full width and stacks above the summary and saved-plans panels, maintaining its role as the first and main focus in the vertical sequence.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The weekly-summary-panel is positioned to the right of the current-plan-week-view within the same horizontal row, inside the shared content container. It starts aligned at the same top baseline as the week view grid, creating a clean horizontal line across the page. On desktop, it occupies approximately one-third of the content container width, spanning about 4 out of 12 columns (around 320–360px), with consistent side padding of 16–24px. The panel itself resembles a calm, compact card: white background, subtle border or shadow, rounded corners, and generous internal padding. Its height is shorter than the full height of the week view, typically about 25–40% of the grid height, so it reads as a summary snapshot rather than a competing main region. Inside, summary metrics or highlights are arranged in a vertical list with clear spacing between items, ensuring the content feels light and easily scannable. The eye naturally drifts to this panel after interacting with the week view, as it is vertically aligned with the top of the grid and visually lighter.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard block within the right-hand column. On long pages, it may remain non-sticky (scrolling away with content) to keep the page feeling calm and non-distracting. On smaller screens, it stacks directly below the week view and above the saved-plans-sidebar, retaining ample top and bottom margins.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The saved-plans-sidebar is placed directly below the weekly-summary-panel within the right-hand column, forming a vertical stack of supporting information. It shares the same right-column width (about 4/12 of the content container) and aligns perfectly with the summary panel’s left and right edges to maintain a clean column.
